task_completion.description: Agent logs into the Jenkins web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins dashboard is visible in a web browser, showing the logged-in user (e.g., 'admin') and the list of jobs.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the configuration page to edit a broken pipeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins 'Configure' page is open for at least one of the target jobs, displaying the Pipeline script editor or configuration settings.

task_completion.description: The payment-service-ci pipeline is fixed and builds successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins interface shows a recent successful build (green checkmark, blue circle, or 'Success' text) in the Build History or Stage View for the 'payment-service-ci' job.

task_completion.description: The user-auth-service pipeline is fixed and builds successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins interface shows a recent successful build (green checkmark, blue circle, or 'Success' text) in the Build History or Stage View for the 'user-auth-service' job.

task_completion.description: The inventory-api-build pipeline is fixed and builds successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins interface shows a recent successful build (green checkmark, blue circle, or 'Success' text) in the Build History or Stage View for the 'inventory-api-build' job.

integrity.description: Agent used the Jenkins web GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Jenkins UI, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Build successes are genuine and not visually spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The successful builds show actual execution details, such as stage views, console output logs, or updated timestamps, rather than manipulated HTML text.

integrity.description: Agent actually modified the pipeline configurations to fix the errors.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen editing the pipeline scripts or environment variables in the Jenkins configuration screens before triggering the successful builds.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target jobs are 'payment-service-ci', 'user-auth-service', and 'inventory-api-build'. The correct credential ID for GitHub access is 'github-access-token'. A successful fix is indicated by a green checkmark or blue success icon in the Jenkins build history for each job. The pass threshold is 60 points (at least 2 jobs fixed).
